description: Reconcile and correct the three affected material transfer records with a documented QA data-integrity assessment against physical stock and ERP movements.
linkedRootCause: Second-person verification ownership for material transfer records was not assigned across the shift-handover window, so records were completed retrospectively without independent verification.
verificationMethod: Record reconciliation and QA data-integrity review

description: Reinstate and document a real-time second-person verification check for material transfers at shift handover with a defined record owner.
linkedRootCause: Second-person verification ownership for material transfer records was not assigned across the shift-handover window, so records were completed retrospectively without independent verification.
verificationMethod: QA review of completed transfer records over two weeks

description: Assess the returned complaint vial and retained samples from lot VAC-26-0419-C for visible particulate and identify the particulate source via visual and microscopy review.
linkedRootCause: Visual inspection rejection trend was not escalated after repeated borderline observations in the same lot family.

description: Re-inspect remaining distributed stock of lot VAC-26-0419-C against the visual-inspection standard and document the QA disposition.
linkedRootCause: Visual inspection rejection trend was not escalated after repeated borderline observations in the same lot family.
verificationMethod: Batch visual-inspection reconciliation and QA disposition review

description: Quarantine the vaccine batches stored in cold room CR-FF-03 during the excursion window and complete a temperature-impact and stability assessment against the product's labelled 2–8 °C storage range.
linkedRootCause: The cold-room compressor's high-temperature alarm was not configured to escalate to the on-call engineer after hours, so a 2–8 °C excursion ran unaddressed until the morning shift.
verificationMethod: Batch quarantine record and QA stability-impact assessment

description: Reconfigure the CR-FF-03 alarm to escalate after-hours high-temperature excursions to the on-call engineer and validate the escalation path with a documented test alarm.
linkedRootCause: The cold-room compressor's high-temperature alarm was not configured to escalate to the on-call engineer after hours, so a 2–8 °C excursion ran unaddressed until the morning shift.
verificationMethod: Alarm escalation test record and on-call response verification

description: Remove the overdue QC balance from service, recalibrate it, and reconcile the calibration status of all QC balances against the master calibration schedule.
linkedRootCause: Calibration due-date tracking for QC balances relied on a manual spreadsheet that was not reconciled against the master calibration schedule, so an overdue balance stayed in service without a flag.
verificationMethod: Calibration certificate review and master-schedule reconciliation

description: Migrate QC calibration due-date tracking from the manual spreadsheet to the centralised calibration register with automated due and overdue flags.
linkedRootCause: Calibration due-date tracking for QC balances relied on a manual spreadsheet that was not reconciled against the master calibration schedule, so an overdue balance stayed in service without a flag.
verificationMethod: Register configuration review and QA verification of automated flags

description: Re-inspect and rework the affected distributed secondary cartons for lot VAC-26-0118-B and confirm barcode scan-grade with the distributor before release for further distribution.
linkedRootCause: Secondary-carton barcode print contrast was not verified against the scan-grade acceptance criteria at line setup, so a low-contrast print batch passed to distribution.
verificationMethod: Carton re-inspection record and distributor scan-grade confirmation

description: Add an ANSI scan-grade barcode verification check at packaging line setup and at defined in-process intervals for the secondary cartoning line.
linkedRootCause: Secondary-carton barcode print contrast was not verified against the scan-grade acceptance criteria at line setup, so a low-contrast print batch passed to distribution.
verificationMethod: In-process barcode verification log review over two packaging campaigns
